{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2004 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2004poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 57th Cannes Film Festival featuring an illustration by ''Alerte Orange''.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2004/posters/off.html |title=Posters 2004 |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214070716/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2004/posters/off.html |archive-date=14 December 2013 |df=dmy}} | host = [[Laura Morante]] | number = 19 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[Bad Education (2004 film)|Bad Education]]'' | closing = ''[[De-Lovely]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Fahrenheit 9/11]]'' | date = {{Start date|2004|05|12|df=y}} – {{End date|2004|05|23|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2003 Cannes Film Festival|2003]] | next = [[2005 Cannes Film Festival|2005]] }} The '''57th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 12 to 23 May 2004. American filmmaker [[Quentin Tarantino]] served as jury president for the main competition. While American filmmaker [[Michael Moore]] won the [[Palme d'Or]] for the documentary film ''[[Fahrenheit 9/11]]'', becoming the first documentary to win the festival's main prize since ''[[The Silent World]]'' in 1956.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2004.htm |title=57ème Festival International du Film - Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |language=fr |access-date=21 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/cine/index.php?a=04 |title=Cannes 2004 / Palmarés |work=cannes-fest.com |language=fr |access-date=21 June 2017}}{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/3742445.stm |title=Cannes 2004 winners in full |work=BBC News |access-date=10 December 2013 |date=24 May 2004 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131102135833/http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/3742445.stm |archive-date=2 November 2013 |df=dmy}} The festival opening film was ''[[Bad Education (2004 film)|Bad Education]]'' directed by [[Pedro Almodóvar]],{{cite web|url=http://www.screendaily.com/almodovars-educacion-to-open-cannes/4017439.article |title=Almodovar's Educacion to open Cannes |work=screendaily.com |access-date=25 May 2017 |quote=needs subscription}} while ''[[De-Lovely]]'' directed by [[Irwin Winkler]] was the closing film.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2004/apr/21/cannes2004.cannesfilmfestival |title=Festival lineup promises Cannes laughter |work=theguardian.com |date=21 April 2004 |access-date=25 May 2017}} [[Laura Morante]] was mistress of the ceremonies.
